message.content: 我在us-east-2有一个volume 5tb，你帮我思考一下怎么同步这个5tb的volume和我现在这台机器上3TB的volume的数据。注意之前有简单的rsync速度只有10MB/s不知道是为什么

addedLines: - claude: Catch-all for any task that doesn't fit a more specific agent. FleetView's default when no agent name is typed. (Tools: *)

- claude-code-guide: Use this agent when the user asks questions ("Can Claude...", "Does Claude...", "How do I...") about: (1) Claude Code (the CLI tool) - features, hooks, slash commands, MCP servers, settings, IDE integrations, keyboard shortcuts; (2) Claude Agent SDK - building custom agents; (3) Claude API (formerly Anthropic API) - Messages API for directly passing messages to Claude, Tool Runner (`client.beta.messages.tool_runner`) for running an agentic loop over your own tools, manual tool-use loops, Managed Agents for server-hosted agents with a managed sandbox, prompt caching, and general Anthropic SDK usage; (4) Claude Tag (Claude in Slack) - what it is, setting it up for a Slack workspace, `/install-slack-app`. **IMPORTANT:** Before spawning a new agent, check if there is already a running or recently completed claude-code-guide agent that you can continue via SendMessage. (Tools: Bash, Read, WebFetch, WebSearch)

- Explore: Fast read-only search agent for locating code. Use it to find files by pattern (eg. "src/components/**/*.tsx"), grep for symbols or keywords (eg. "API endpoints"), or answer "where is X defined / which files reference Y." Do NOT use it for code review, design-doc auditing, cross-file consistency checks, or open-ended analysis — it reads excerpts rather than whole files and will miss content past its read window. When calling, specify search breadth: "quick" for a single targeted lookup, "medium" for moderate exploration, or "very thorough" to search across multiple locations and naming conventions. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)

- general-purpose: General-purpose agent for researching complex questions, searching for code, and executing multi-step tasks. When you are searching for a keyword or file and are not confident that you will find the right match in the first few tries use this agent to perform the search for you. (Tools: *)

- Plan: Software architect agent for designing implementation plans. Use this when you need to plan the implementation strategy for a task. Returns step-by-step plans, identifies critical files, and considers architectural trade-offs. (Tools: All tools except Agent, Artifact, ExitPlanMode, Edit, Write, NotebookEdit)
- statusline-setup: Use this agent to configure the user's Claude Code status line setting. (Tools: Read, Edit)

attachment.content:
- caveman: Ultra-compressed communication mode. Cuts token usage ~75% by dropping filler, articles, and pleasantries while keeping full technical accuracy. Use when user says "caveman mode", "talk like caveman", "use caveman", "less tokens", "be brief", or invokes /caveman.
- diagnose: Disciplined diagnosis loop for hard bugs and performance regressions. Reproduce → minimise → hypothesise → instrument → fix → regression-test. Use when user says "diagnose this" / "debug this", reports a bug, says something is broken/throwing/failing, or describes a performance regression.
- grill-me: Interview the user relentlessly about a plan or design until reaching shared understanding, resolving each branch of the decision tree. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan, get grilled on their design, or mentions "grill me".
- grill-with-docs: Grilling session that challenges your plan against the existing domain model, sharpens terminology, and updates documentation (CONTEXT.md, ADRs) inline as decisions crystallise. Use when user wants to stress-test a plan against their project's language and documented decisions.
- handoff: Compact the current conversation into a handoff document for another agent to pick up.
- improve-codebase-architecture: Find deepening opportunities in a codebase, informed by the domain language in CONTEXT.md and the decisions in docs/adr/. Use when the user wants to improve architecture, find refactoring opportunities, consolidate tightly-coupled modules, or make a codebase more testable and AI-navigable.
- prototype: Build a throwaway prototype to flesh out a design before committing to it. Routes between two branches — a runnable terminal app for state/business-logic questions, or several radically different UI variations toggleable from one route. Use when the user wants to prototype, sanity-check a data model or state machine, mock up a UI, explore design options, or says "prototype this", "let me play with it", "try a few designs".
- tdd: Test-driven development with red-green-refactor loop. Use when user wants to build features or fix bugs using TDD, mentions "red-green-refactor", wants integration tests, or asks for test-first development.
- to-prd: Turn the current conversation context into a PRD and publish it to the project issue tracker. Use when user wants to create a PRD from the current context.
- triage: Triage issues through a state machine driven by triage roles. Use when user wants to create an issue, triage issues, review incoming bugs or feature requests, prepare issues for an AFK agent, or manage issue workflow.
- write-a-skill: Create new agent skills with proper structure, progressive disclosure, and bundled resources. Use when user wants to create, write, or build a new skill.
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- dataviz: Use this skill whenever you are about to create ANY chart, graph, plot, dashboard, or data visualization, in ANY output medium — an HTML or React artifact, inline SVG, plotting code in any library (matplotlib, plotly, d3, Recharts, …), an image/PNG you will render and upload, or a chart shared into Slack. Read it BEFORE writing the first line of chart code, choosing chart colors, building a stat tile / meter / KPI row, or laying out a dashboard. Produces visualizations that read as one system — elegant, accessible, consistent in light and dark — using a brand-neutral placeholder palette you swap for your own. Teaches a design-system-agnostic method: a form heuristic, a color formula with a runnable validator, mark specs, and interaction rules. A validated default palette is documented in `references/palette.md` — swap that file's values for your brand's. Triggers on: "chart", "graph", "plot", "data viz", "visualization", "dashboard", "analytics", "visualize data", "categorical colors", "sequential / diverging palette", "stat tile", "sparkline", "heatmap", "legend", "axis", "tooltip", "chart colors", "color by series".
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo, defaults to 10m) - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

content.thinking:
Let me gather more info about the network bandwidth and current setup.

The user has:
- Current machine: likely in some AWS region (can't determine exactly from metadata), with a 3TB EBS volume (`/dev/nvme2n1` mounted at `/dev3`), 12 vCPUs, 124GB RAM, Intel Xeon 8488C (likely a `r7i.4xlarge` or similar), ENA driver with MTU 9001 (jumbo frames enabled).
- Remote machine in us-east-2: has a 5TB volume.
- Previous rsync speed: only 10MB/s, which is very slow.
- The 10MB/s bottleneck likely stems from rsync being single-threaded over SSH, which can't saturate the pipe when there's cross-region latency involved. EBS throughput limits and SSH encryption overhead could also be factors, though the real issue is probably that a single TCP stream can't fill the available bandwidth across regions.

Now I'm trying to figure out what region this instance is in to better understand the constraints.

input.command: curl -s --connect-timeout 2 http://169.254.169.254/latest/dynamic/instance-identity/document 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('region','?'), d.get('instanceType','?'), d.get('availabilityZone','?'))" 2>/dev/null || echo "metadata unavailable"

input.command: TOKEN=$(curl -s -X PUT "http://169.254.169.254/latest/api/token" -H "X-aws-ec2-metadata-token-ttl-seconds: 21600" 2>/dev/null) && curl -s -H "X-aws-ec2-metadata-token: $TOKEN" http://169.254.169.254/latest/dynamic/instance-identity/document 2>/dev/null | python3 -c "import sys,json; d=json.load(sys.stdin); print(d.get('region'), d.get('instanceType'), d.get('availabilityZone'))"
input.description: Get instance metadata with IMDSv2 token

lastPrompt: 我在us-east-2有一个volume 5tb，你帮我思考一下怎么同步这个5tb的volume和我现在这台机器上3TB的volume的数据。注意之前有简单的rsync速度只有10MB/s不知道是为什么

aiTitle: 优化跨区域5TB volume数据同步速度

content.text:
好的，现在情况很清楚了。这是你的环境：

## 当前机器

- **Region**: `ap-southeast-4` (Melbourne)
- **Instance**: `trn2.3xlarge` (12 vCPU, 124GB RAM)
- **Network**: ENA driver, MTU 9001 (Jumbo frames enabled)
- **Volume**: 3TB (`/dev3`), 已用 789GB
- **磁盘 IO**: NVMe

## 为什么 rsync 只有 10MB/s？

跨 region（ap-southeast-4 → us-east-2）的问题：

1. **跨洲际延迟**：Melbourne → Ohio 大概 200-250ms RTT。rsync 是**单流串行传输**，每个文件传完才传下一个，高延迟下吞吐量被 TCP 窗口严重限制。
2. **单 TCP 流瓶颈**：即使带宽充足（trn2.3xlarge 有 32 Gbps 网络），单个 TCP 连接在 200ms RTT 下理论最大吞吐也就 ~50-100 MB/s（取决于 TCP buffer），但 rsync 的协议开销让它更低。
3. **小文件多**：如果有大量小文件，rsync 的 per-file 握手开销在高延迟下被放大。

## 推荐方案（按优先级排序）

### 方案 1：多流并行 rsync（最简单，预计 100-300 MB/s）

```bash
# 安装 parallel
sudo apt-get install -y parallel

# 先生成文件列表
find /dev3 -type f > /tmp/file_list.txt

# 用 GNU parallel 开 16-32 个并行 rsync 流
cat /tmp/file_list.txt | parallel -j 16 --bar \
  rsync -avz --progress -e "ssh -T -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com -o Compression=no" \
  {} user@us-east-2-host:/destination/{}
```

或者更好的方式——按目录分片：
```bash
# 按顶层目录并行
ls /dev3 | parallel -j 16 \
  rsync -av --progress -e "ssh -T -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com -o Compression=no" \
  /dev3/{} user@us-east-2-host:/destination/
```

### 方案 2：`rclone` 多线程传输（推荐，简单且快）

```bash
# 安装 rclone
curl https://rclone.org/install.sh | sudo bash

# 配置 SFTP remote
rclone config
# 选 sftp, 填目标机器 IP/key

# 多线程同步（16 并行传输，每个文件多线程）
rclone sync /dev3 remote:/destination \
  --transfers 32 \
  --checkers 16 \
  --multi-thread-streams 4 \
  --progress
```

### 方案 3：S3 中转（最稳定，适合 5TB 规模）

如果两边都能访问 S3：
```bash
# 源端上传到 S3 (ap-southeast-4 或 us-east-2 都行)
aws s3 sync /dev3 s3://your-bucket/sync/ --storage-class STANDARD \
  --expected-size 5000000000000

# 目标端下载
aws s3 sync s3://your-bucket/sync/ /destination/
```
优势：AWS 内网 S3 传输极快，且自带断点续传和校验。

### 方案 4：`bbcp` 或 `aspera`（专为高延迟大文件设计）

```bash
# bbcp - 并行 TCP 流，大 buffer
bbcp -P 25 -s 16 -w 4M /dev3/* user@remote:/destination/
# -s 16: 16个并行流
# -w 4M: 4MB TCP window
```

## 关键调优（无论哪种方案都做）

```bash
# 在两端都设置大 TCP buffer (临时)
sudo sysctl -w net.core.rmem_max=67108864
sudo sysctl -w net.core.wmem_max=67108864
sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.tcp_rmem="4096 87380 33554432"
sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.tcp_wmem="4096 65536 33554432"
sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.tcp_window_scaling=1
sudo sysctl -w net.core.netdev_max_backlog=30000

# SSH 用快加密 + 禁压缩（如果数据本身不可压缩）
ssh -T -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com -o Compression=no
```

## 我的建议

对你这个 5TB 跨洲际的场景，**方案 3（S3 中转）** 最合适：
- 断点续传免费
- AWS 骨干网比公网快得多
- 不需要两台机器同时在线
- 传输速度通常能到 500MB/s+（单机器到 S3）

如果不想用 S3，**rclone 多流 SFTP（方案 2）** 是最简单的升级，预计能从 10MB/s 提到 100-200 MB/s。

需要我帮你具体配置哪个方案？

message.content: S3中转用的是什么技术？中间节点是哪？也是一台EC2服务器吗

content.thinking:
The user is asking about the S3 transit approach - what technology it uses, where the intermediate node is, and whether it's an EC2 server.

Let me explain how S3 works as a transit mechanism for cross-region data transfer.

content.text:
不是 EC2 服务器。S3 是 AWS 的对象存储服务，本质上是一个分布式存储集群。

## S3 中转的原理

```
你的机器 (ap-southeast-4)  →  S3 Bucket  →  目标机器 (us-east-2)
        上传                    存储               下载
```

**S3 不是一台服务器**，而是 AWS 在每个 region 部署的大规模分布式存储系统（数千台存储节点组成）。你的数据直接写入 S3 的存储层，没有"中间服务器"在转发。

## 为什么比 rsync 快

| 维度 | rsync (机器→机器) | S3 中转 |
|------|-------------------|---------|
| 连接 | 单 TCP 流，跨洲 200ms RTT | 机器到同 region S3 端点，<2ms RTT |
| 并行 | 默认 1 流 | `aws s3 sync` 自动多线程（默认 10 并发） |
| 协议 | SSH 封装，per-file 握手 | HTTP/2 multipart upload，大文件自动分片 |
| 断点 | 中断需重来 | multipart upload 自动续传 |

关键点：**每一段传输都是 region 内的短距离**（机器到同 region 的 S3 endpoint），延迟极低。跨 region 的"远距离"部分由 AWS 骨干网在 S3 复制层完成（如果你用 S3 Cross-Region Replication），或者干脆就在目标 region 建 bucket，让目标机器从同 region S3 下载。

## 两种做法

### 做法 A：单 bucket，两端各自上传/下载（简单）

```bash
# 在 us-east-2 建 bucket（离目标机器近）
aws s3 mb s3://my-sync-bucket --region us-east-2

# 源端上传（ap-southeast-4 → us-east-2 S3，走 AWS 骨干网）
aws s3 sync /dev3 s3://my-sync-bucket/ --region us-east-2

# 目标端下载（us-east-2 机器 → 同 region S3，极快）
aws s3 sync s3://my-sync-bucket/ /destination/
```

这里上传段仍然跨洲，但 `aws s3 sync` 会自动用 multipart upload（大文件切成 8-64MB 分片并行传），所以即使延迟高，吞吐量也能打满带宽。

### 做法 B：两端各自建 bucket + S3 Replication（最快）

```bash
# ap-southeast-4 建源 bucket
aws s3 mb s3://sync-source --region ap-southeast-4

# us-east-2 建目标 bucket  
aws s3 mb s3://sync-dest --region us-east-2

# 配置 Cross-Region Replication (CRR)
# 源端上传到本地 bucket（<2ms 延迟，极快）
aws s3 sync /dev3 s3://sync-source/

# AWS 自动在骨干网上复制到 us-east-2 的 bucket
# 目标机器从本地 bucket 下载
aws s3 sync s3://sync-dest/ /destination/
```

这样两端都是 region 内传输，跨洲部分完全由 AWS 内部骨干网处理。

## 费用参考（5TB）

- S3 存储：~$115/月（Standard，用完可删）
- 跨 region 传输：~$0.02/GB = **~$100 一次性**
- 同 region EC2→S3 上传/下载：免费

总计大约 $100-200 传一次 5TB。如果只是一次性同步，传完删 bucket 就行。

需要我帮你配置具体的 `aws s3 sync` 命令吗？或者你更倾向于不花钱的多流 rsync/rclone 方案？

message.content: S3存储要立刻付一个月的钱吗

content.thinking: The user is asking whether S3 storage requires paying for a full month upfront.

content.text:
不用。S3 是**按实际使用量按秒计费**的，不需要预付。

- 存 1 天就付 1 天的钱
- 存 1 小时就付 1 小时的钱
- 传完数据、目标机器下载完、你删掉 bucket 里的文件，计费立刻停止

## 算一下你的场景

假设 5TB 数据在 S3 上停留 3 天（上传→下载→删除）：

| 项目 | 费用 |
|------|------|
| 存储 | 5TB × $0.023/GB/月 × (3/30) = **~$11** |
| PUT 请求（上传） | 假设 100 万文件 × $0.005/1000 = **~$5** |
| GET 请求（下载） | 100 万文件 × $0.0004/1000 = **~$0.4** |
| 跨 region 数据传出 | 5TB × $0.02/GB = **~$100** |

总计约 **$115**，其中大头是跨 region 传输费（$100）。存储本身几乎可以忽略。

如果你用 **做法 B**（源端 bucket 在 ap-southeast-4，目标 bucket 在 us-east-2，用 CRR），跨 region 传输费是一样的，但两端上传/下载都是免费的（同 region EC2↔S3 不收传输费）。

## 省钱替代

如果不想花这 $100 跨 region 费：
- **rclone 多流 SFTP**：免费，预计 100-200 MB/s，5TB 大约 7-14 小时
- **多流 rsync（parallel）**：免费，类似速度

要不要试试免费的 rclone 方案？我可以直接帮你配。

message.content: 我有很多小文件怎么办？

content.thinking:
The user is asking about how to handle many small files in the data transfer scenario. Small files are a known problem for cross-region transfers because:

1. Per-file overhead (SSH handshake, S3 PUT requests, etc.)
2. High latency amplifies per-file overhead
3. S3 charges per-request, so millions of small files = expensive PUT/GET costs

Let me give practical advice on handling this.

content.text:
小文件是跨洲传输的最大性能杀手。核心思路：**先打包再传输**。

## 问题分析

假设你有 500 万个小文件（平均 1MB）：

| 方案 | 小文件的问题 |
|------|-------------|
| rsync | 每个文件一次 RTT 握手，500 万 × 200ms = 永远传不完 |
| S3 | 500 万次 PUT = **$25 请求费**，且每个 PUT 有固定开销 |
| rclone | 比 rsync 好，但仍有 per-file HTTP 开销 |

## 最佳方案：tar 打包 + 并行传输

### 做法 1：tar 流式传输（不落盘，最省空间）

```bash
# 直接 tar 流过 SSH，不需要本地额外空间
tar -cf - /dev3/some_dir | \
  ssh -T -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com user@us-east-2-host \
  "tar -xf - -C /destination/"
```

500 万小文件变成 1 个连续流，完全消除 per-file 开销。但仍是单流。

### 做法 2：分目录并行 tar（推荐）

```bash
# 按子目录并行打包传输，16 路并发
ls /dev3 | parallel -j 16 \
  'tar -cf - /dev3/{} | ssh -T -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com user@remote "tar -xf - -C /destination/"'
```

### 做法 3：先本地 tar.zst 打包，再多流传输（最快最稳）

```bash
# 第一步：按目录打包成大文件（zstd 压缩快，CPU 开销小）
ls /dev3 | parallel -j 8 \
  'tar -cf - /dev3/{} | zstd -1 -T4 > /tmp/pack_{}.tar.zst'

# 第二步：传输少量大文件（rclone/rsync 传大文件很快）
rclone sync /tmp/pack_*.tar.zst remote:/tmp/ --transfers 16

# 第三步：目标端解压
ssh remote 'ls /tmp/pack_*.tar.zst | parallel -j 8 "zstd -d {} --stdout | tar -xf - -C /destination/"'
```

但这需要额外的本地磁盘空间。你目前 /dev3 还有 2TB 空闲，可能够用。

### 做法 4：S3 + tar 打包（省请求费）

```bash
# 打包成大文件再传 S3，PUT 请求从 500万 降到几百
ls /dev3 | parallel -j 8 \
  'tar -cf - /dev3/{} | zstd -1 -T2 | \
   aws s3 cp - s3://my-bucket/packs/{}.tar.zst --expected-size 10000000000'

# 目标端
aws s3 ls s3://my-bucket/packs/ | awk '{print $4}' | parallel -j 8 \
  'aws s3 cp s3://my-bucket/packs/{} - | zstd -d | tar -xf - -C /destination/'
```

S3 请求费从 $25 降到几毛钱。

## 我的建议

| 你的情况 | 推荐 |
|---------|------|
| 不想花钱、目标机器能 SSH | **做法 2**（并行 tar over SSH） |
| 想要断点续传、怕中断 | **做法 3**（先打包再用 rclone 传） |
| 追求最快最稳 | **做法 4**（tar + S3） |

你大概知道你的小文件有多少个、平均多大吗？我可以帮你算一下具体用几路并发最合适。

echo "=== File size distribution (sample 10000 files) ===" && find /dev3 -type f 2>/dev/null | shuf -n 10000 | xargs -d'\n' stat --format='%s' 2>/dev/null | awk '
BEGIN {s0=0; s1k=0; s10k=0; s100k=0; s1m=0; s10m=0; s100m=0; s1g=0; big=0}
{
  if ($1 == 0) s0++
  else if ($1 < 1024) s1k++
  else if ($1 < 10240) s10k++
  else if ($1 < 102400) s100k++
  else if ($1 < 1048576) s1m++
  else if ($1 < 10485760) s10m++
  else if ($1 < 104857600) s100m++
  else if ($1 < 1073741824) s1g++
  else big++
}
END {
  total = s0+s1k+s10k+s100k+s1m+s10m+s100m+s1g+big
  printf "%-15s %6d (%5.1f%%)\n", "0 bytes", s0, s0/total*100
  printf "%-15s %6d (%5.1f%%)\n", "1B - 1KB", s1k, s1k/total*100
  printf "%-15s %6d (%5.1f%%)\n", "1KB - 10KB", s10k, s10k/total*100
  printf "%-15s %6d (%5.1f%%)\n", "10KB - 100KB", s100k, s100k/total*100
  printf "%-15s %6d (%5.1f%%)\n", "100KB - 1MB", s1m, s1m/total*100
  printf "%-15s %6d (%5.1f%%)\n", "1MB - 10MB", s10m, s10m/total*100
  printf "%-15s %6d (%5.1f%%)\n", "10MB - 100MB", s100m, s100m/total*100
  printf "%-15s %6d (%5.1f%%)\n", "100MB - 1GB", s1g, s1g/total*100
  printf "%-15s %6d (%5.1f%%)\n", ">1GB", big, big/total*100
}'

content.text:
好的，数据画像很清晰了。总结一下：

## 你的数据特征

| 指标 | 数值 |
|------|------|
| **总文件数** | ~64 万 |
| **总大小** | ~789 GB（已用） |
| **文件大小分布** | 82% 的文件 < 100KB（典型小文件） |
| **最大单文件** | 302GB（`indexes/reason-moderncolbert/index.voyager`） |

### 按目录分层

| 目录 | 大小 | 文件数 | 特征 |
|------|------|--------|------|
| `BrowseComp-Plus/indexes/` | 325G | 少量超大文件 | 单文件 302GB |
| `server-backups/` | 221G | 23 个 | 已经是 20GB 分片 tar.zst |
| `BrowseComp-Plus/local-models/` | 114G | 少量大文件 | 模型权重 |
| `BrowseComp-Plus/runs/` | 13G | 大量 json | 小文件为主 |
| `ast-visualizer/` | 21G | 12 万 | 小文件多 |
| `restored-server/` | 47G | 26 万 | 小文件多 |

### 文件类型

主要是 `.json`、`.py`、`.pyc`、`.h`、`.npy`、`.svg`、`.png` — 大部分是文本/代码/小二进制。

## 针对性传输策略

你的数据天然分成两类，应该**分开处理**：

### 第一类：少量大文件（~660G，<100个文件）

直接 rclone/rsync 传就行，单文件 302GB 能跑满带宽：

```bash
# indexes + local-models + server-backups（都是大文件）
rclone sync /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/indexes remote:/destination/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/indexes \
  --transfers 4 --multi-thread-streams 8 --progress

rclone sync /dev3/server-backups remote:/destination/server-backups \
  --transfers 4 --progress
```

### 第二类：海量小文件（~130G，63万个文件）

**必须打包**，否则 per-file 开销会杀死性能：

```bash
# runs/ (13G, 大量json) → 打包
tar -cf - /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/runs | zstd -1 -T8 | \
  ssh -T -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com user@remote "zstd -d | tar -xf - -C /destination/"

# ast-visualizer (21G, 12万文件) → 打包
tar -cf - /dev3/zigeng/ast-visualizer | zstd -1 -T8 | \
  ssh -T -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com user@remote "zstd -d | tar -xf - -C /destination/"

# restored-server (47G, 26万文件) → 打包
tar -cf - /dev3/restored-server | zstd -1 -T8 | \
  ssh -T -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com user@remote "zstd -d | tar -xf - -C /destination/"
```

### 完整方案脚本

```bash
#!/bin/bash
REMOTE="user@us-east-2-host"
DEST="/destination"
SSH_OPTS="-T -c aes128-gcm@openssh.com -o Compression=no"

# === 大文件：直接多流传 ===
rclone sync /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/indexes $REMOTE:$DEST/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/indexes \
  --transfers 4 --multi-thread-streams 8 --progress &

rclone sync /dev3/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models $REMOTE:$DEST/zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/local-models \
  --transfers 4 --multi-thread-streams 4 --progress &

rclone sync /dev3/server-backups $REMOTE:$DEST/server-backups \
  --transfers 4 --progress &

# === 小文件：tar 流式传输（并行 4 路）===
for dir in "zigeng/BrowseComp-Plus/runs" "zigeng/ast-visualizer" "restored-server" "zigeng/uv-cache"; do
  tar -cf - "/dev3/$dir" | zstd -1 -T4 | \
    ssh $SSH_OPTS $REMOTE "zstd -d | tar -xf - -C $DEST/" &
done

wait
echo "Done"
```

**预期速度**：大文件部分 100-300 MB/s，小文件打包后也能达到类似速度。总计 789GB 大约 **1-2 小时**可以传完（取决于跨洲际带宽实际瓶颈）。

content.text:
你本地没有 `zigeng-key` 的私钥文件。你知道私钥存在哪里吗？或者我可以：

1. **创建一个新的 key pair**，下载到本地
2. 或者你告诉我私钥路径

questions.question: 你本地没有 zigeng-key 私钥。怎么处理？

input.command:
cat > ~/.ssh/zigeng-key << 'EOF'
-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
b3BlbnNzaC1rZXktdjEAAAAABG5vbmUAAAAEbm9uZQAAAAAAAAABAAAAMwAAAAtzc2gtZW
QyNTUxOQAAACBeOOUIf3E1jAFZcMMjVPd9KZ8p4E7rMAGx0Jx1DYNGfQAAAKC8gELavIBC
2gAAAAtzc2gtZWQyNTUxOQAAACBeOOUIf3E1jAFZcMMjVPd9KZ8p4E7rMAGx0Jx1DYNGfQ
AAAECi7N943BI9QnAUWTBh1CLtsja3QQe5IncE65EWv1KurV445Qh/cTWMAVlwwyNU930p
nyngTuswAbHQnHUNg0Z9AAAAGWFiYzE1OTcxNzY2NjhAb3V0bG9vay5jb20BAgME
-----END OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----
EOF
chmod 600 ~/.ssh/zigeng-key
input.description: Save private key to file with correct permissions

input.command: ssh -o StrictHostKeyChecking=no -o ConnectTimeout=10 -i ~/.ssh/zigeng-key ubuntu@3.139.58.108 "echo 'SSH OK'; uname -a"
input.description: Test SSH with the key

input.command:
# Test 1: latency (RTT)
ping -c 5 3.139.58.108
input.description: Measure RTT to us-east-2

input.command:
# Install iperf3 on remote
ssh -i ~/.ssh/zigeng-key ubuntu@3.139.58.108 "sudo apt-get update -qq && sudo apt-get install -y -qq iperf3 2>/dev/null; echo done"
input.description: Install iperf3 on remote for bandwidth test
